ocr.text: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of genetic variants associated with longevity and disease
susceptibility, specifically focusing on the cohort of individuals under study. Notably, the Disease ID column allows for the
identification of specific genetic conditions linked to the observed variants, while the Individuals with variants column
highlights the prevalence of these genetic markers among the population. The analysis reveals that out of a total of 216
individuals, a significant 72.2% (156 individuals) carry variants classified as ONDRISeq variants, underscoring the genetic
diversity within this cohort. Furthermore, the metrics concerning Variants in disease gene as diagnosed and Variants in other
ONDRISeq disease genes provide insight into potential correlations between these genetic factors and various health outcomes.
For instance, the gene APOE, specifically the ε4* variant, is noted for its association with reduced longevity, reinforcing the
need for further investigation into its role in age-related diseases. Overall, this table not only elucidates the genetic landscape of
the studied population but also emphasizes the intricate relationship between genetic variants and their implications for health
and longevity.

Disease ID  Individuals with variants  ONDRISeq variants  Variants in disease gene as diagnosed  Variants in other ONDRISeq disease genes  Variants in disease databases  Variants not found in disease databases
Gene  Variant  Occurrence in centenarians  Previous disease correlations  Potential role in longevity  Ref.
